title: Simpliglottis grammata
taxonomy: Simpliglottis grammata is classified as a species of orchid, a flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its morphology and reproductive structures. Specifically, Simpliglottis grammata is classified within the genus Simpliglottis, a group of orchids native to the tropical regions of Asia. Further, it is often grouped under the subtribe Aeridinae, which includes several other species of orchids. The classification of Simpliglottis grammata reflects its unique combination of characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species.

intro: Simpliglottis grammata, also known as the "Himalayan Orchid", is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Simpliglottis grammata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Simpliglottis grammata is often prepared as a decoction or extract, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
